days.title: Arrival in Tokyo
days.subtitle: Landing in the neon metropolis

timeline.title: Narita Express Train Departure
timeline.detail: Board the Narita Express (N'EX) train from Narita International Airport Terminal 1 or 2. The N'EX is the most convenient transport option for your company group of 6, offering direct connections to central Tokyo. Purchase a group discount ticket at the airport station. The journey to Shinjuku Station takes approximately 60 minutes with comfortable seating and luggage space.
timeline.mapQuery: Narita Express, Narita International Airport, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Arrival at Shinjuku Station
timeline.detail: Arrive at JR Shinjuku Station, one of Tokyo's busiest transport hubs. Organize the group and identify the best exit for Mitsui Garden Hotel Shinjuku. Take photos of the iconic station signage and electric billboards. Note the station layout for evening navigation. Allow 15 minutes to orient the group before heading to the hotel.

timeline.title: Hotel Check-in at Mitsui Garden Hotel Shinjuku
timeline.detail: Check in to Mitsui Garden Hotel Shinjuku, a 4-star property perfectly located in the heart of Shinjuku. Inform the front desk about your company group of 6 and request room assignments. Ask for restaurant and transportation recommendations. The hotel offers modern rooms with city views and excellent access to shopping, dining, and nightlife venues throughout the district.
timeline.mapQuery: Mitsui Garden Hotel Shinjuku, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Lunch at Ramen Alley
timeline.detail: Take the company group to Ramen Yokocho (Ramen Alley), a hidden gem with 8 small traditional ramen shops just minutes from the hotel. Each shop specializes in different ramen styles from across Japan. It's perfect for a company outing as the casual seating encourages conversation and team bonding. Budget approximately 1,200-1,500 yen per person for authentic Shinjuku ramen experience.

timeline.title: Shinjuku Shopping and Photography Walk
timeline.detail: Explore Shinjuku's famous shopping districts including Meiji-dori and Omesan-dori streets. Photograph the vibrant storefronts, neon signs, and electric billboards that define Tokyo's modern aesthetic. Visit department stores like Takashimaya or Mitsukoshi where the company group can shop for souvenirs. The area is densely packed with fashion boutiques, electronics stores, and specialty shops catering to diverse interests.

timeline.title: Rest and Refresh at Hotel
timeline.detail: Return to Mitsui Garden Hotel Shinjuku to rest after arrival and morning activities. The group can freshen up in their rooms and prepare for the evening. This allows time to organize belongings, confirm dinner reservations, and charge photography equipment and mobile devices before heading out for the nightlife portion of Day 1.
timeline.mapQuery: Mitsui Garden Hotel Shinjuku, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Dinner at Izakaya Establishment
timeline.detail: Enjoy a traditional Japanese izakaya dinner as a company group, featuring small plates, grilled skewers, fresh seafood, and local sake and beer selections. Izakayas are perfect for team bonding with their communal dining style and relaxed atmosphere. Choose a location in Shinjuku like Memory Lane Omoide Yokocho where multiple izakayas offer private or semi-private seating. Budget 3,000-5,000 yen per person.

timeline.title: Shinjuku Nightlife and Neon City Exploration
timeline.detail: Explore Shinjuku's famous nightlife district featuring illuminated pachinko parlors, karaoke establishments, and neon-lit streets. Walk through Kabukicho to experience Tokyo's vibrant after-dark energy and photograph the spectacular LED displays and street signs. The company group can stop at a karaoke bar for team entertainment or visit a themed bar. The entire district transforms into a glittering spectacle perfect for photography.

timeline.title: Return to Hotel and Night Wind Down
timeline.detail: Head back to Mitsui Garden Hotel Shinjuku for the evening. The hotel is just a short walk from Kabukicho and major nightlife areas. The group can enjoy the hotel's facilities including the guest lounge or retire to rooms. Review Day 1 highlights and plan Day 2 activities. Ensure adequate rest for the upcoming days of the Tokyo company adventure.
timeline.mapQuery: Mitsui Garden Hotel Shinjuku, Tokyo, Japan

localTips: Purchase Suica/Pasmo IC card at Narita Express counter — essential for all transport in Tokyo (trains, buses, convenience stores)
Shinjuku is the busiest station in the world; get a station map and identify your hotel exit before arrival
Convenience stores (Lawson, 7-Eleven, FamilyMart) are everywhere — grab snacks and drinks 24/7
Most restaurants in Shinjuku don't have English menus; use Google Translate camera feature or point at photos
Tax refund is available for foreign visitors on purchases over ¥5,000 at department stores — bring passport

days.title: Shinjuku & Electric Town
days.subtitle: Neon nights and urban exploration
localTips: Akihabara is packed on weekends — visit on weekday morning for better photos and shorter queues at electronics stores
Don Quijote (mega discount store) in Akihabara has 8 floors of souvenirs, gadgets, and duty-free goods
Shinjuku Gyoen is free entry and stunning during cherry blossom season — arrive before 10:00 AM to avoid crowds
Memory Lane (Omoide Yokocho) is a narrow alley of tiny yakitori restaurants — each seats 5-6 people, perfect for group bonding
JR East Pass is NOT valid in Tokyo — use your Suica card for all trains and buses

days.title: Asakusa & Traditional Tokyo
days.subtitle: Ancient temples and street food
localTips: Arrive at Senso-ji Temple before 07:00 AM for quiet photos and shorter prayer lines
Nakamise street has 89 souvenir shops selling traditional crafts, fans, and snacks — allow 1.5 hours to browse
Hoppy Street (Hoppy Dori) near Asakusa has nostalgic 1950s izakayas — book ahead for dinner with group
Sumida River boat cruises run every 15 minutes from Asakusa Pier — scenic route to Odaiba (35 min journey)
East exit of Asakusa Station is chaotic; use West exit and walk through quieter streets to reach temple

days.title: Odaiba Modern Island
days.subtitle: Futuristic architecture and shopping
localTips: teamLab Borderless tickets MUST be pre-booked online at teamlab.art — walk-in entry is not permitted and daily capacity is limited to 3,000 visitors
Diver City Tokyo has giant Gundam statue (free to view) and 6 floors of shopping — quieter than central Tokyo malls
Odaiba is connected by automated Yurikamome monorail from Shimbashi Station (5 min journey, ¥230 per person)
Palette Town has a 60-meter Ferris wheel offering 360° views of Tokyo Bay — queue times peak 15:00-18:00
Tsukiji Outer Market (moved to Toyosu) has fresh sushi breakfast — take early morning Yurikamome from your hotel

days.title: Shibuya & Harajuku Styles
days.subtitle: Fashion, culture, and Scramble Crossing
localTips: Shibuya Crossing is busiest during evening rush (17:00-19:00) and rainy days — shoot from Starbucks 3rd floor or the corner above crossing
Meiji Shrine is surrounded by a peaceful 1,500-tree forest — morning visit (before 08:00) offers peaceful photos without crowds
Takeshita Street becomes pedestrian-only after 18:00 and is packed with tourists — shop early morning (10:00-12:00) for better experience
Omotesando is Tokyo's answer to Paris's Champs-Élysées — luxury boutiques and museums line both sides
Yoyogi Park opposite Meiji Shrine is perfect for group picnics and people-watching — convenience store snacks are cheap and excellent

days.title: Mount Fuji & Hakone Day Trip
days.subtitle: Alpine views and mountain scenery
localTips: Book Hakone round-trip express buses from Tokyo Station Yaesu exit 5 days in advance — they sell out during peak season
Mount Fuji visibility is highest in early morning and after rain — check weather forecast and webcams before booking the day trip
Lake Ashi pirate ship cruise takes 30 minutes one way — a must-do for photos with Fuji in the background
Hakone Open Air Museum has 120 sculptures in an outdoor setting with mountain vistas — allow 2 hours minimum for photography
Hakone has natural hot springs (onsen) — book a resort overnight to experience authentic bathing culture, though day trip is feasible

days.title: Departure Day & Last-Minute Shopping
days.subtitle: Final memories and airport transfer
localTips: Ginza has flagship stores of all major luxury and Japanese brands — tax refunds available with passport for foreign visitors
Leave hotel by 13:00 at latest to catch 15:00 or 16:00 Narita Express back to airport (60 min journey)
Narita Express departs from major terminals: Tokyo, Shinjuku, Shibuya, Ikebukuro — confirm which station your hotel is closest to
Airport departure tax (usually ¥1,000) is included in airline tickets for international flights
Duty-free shops at Narita have unique Japanese brands (Shiseido, Sake, KitKat flavors) — arrive at airport 3 hours early

hotels.name: Mitsui Garden Hotel Shinjuku
hotels.note: Direct connection to JR Shinjuku Station East Exit, modern 4-star comfort

budget.item: Flights: Penang to Tokyo return (6 passengers)
budget.note: Estimate RM 3,000 per person via AirAsia/MAS/Jetstar or regional carrier

budget.item: Hotel: Mitsui Garden Shinjuku (6 nights × 6 rooms)
budget.note: ¥18,000/night (~RM 560) × 6 nights × 6 rooms. Near station, excellent for company groups

budget.item: Suica/Pasmo IC cards (6 cards + ¥2,000 load per person)

budget.note: ¥3,200 per adult (~RM 280) × 6 = pre-book at teamlab.art before trip
¥5,000 per person (~RM 240) from Tokyo Station, includes Lake Ashi pirate ship

budget.item: Meals: Breakfast buffets, lunches, dinners (6 people × 7 days)
budget.note: Estimate ¥2,000-3,000/day per person (~RM 50-75). Mix of ramen, sushi, izakaya, convenience stores

budget.item: Senso-ji Temple entry + Nakamise shopping (6 people)
budget.note: Senso-ji free entry. Souvenir shopping budget ~RM 100/person for fans, omamori, crafts

budget.item: Shinjuku Gyoen National Garden entry (6 people)
budget.note: ¥500 per person (~RM 15) × 6 = free for kids under 12

budget.item: Yoyogi Park picnic + Meiji Shrine area exploration
budget.note: Convenience store snacks and drinks ¥500-800 per person for group picnic. Meiji Shrine is free

budget.item: Harajuku Takeshita Street shopping & fashion (6 people)
budget.note: Budget RM 400/person for trendy clothing, accessories, street food crepes

budget.item: Ginza shopping & luxury brands duty-free (6 people)
budget.note: Budget RM 700/person for cosmetics, sake, KitKat varieties, final souvenirs. Tax refunds available

budget.item: Nightlife: Karaoke, izakaya group dinners, bars (6 people × 3 nights)
budget.note: Karaoke ¥1,000/hr (~RM 30), izakaya ¥3,000-5,000 per person (~RM 90-150). Perfect company bonding

urgent.label: Pre-book teamLab Borderless tickets
urgent.note: Daily capacity limited to 3,000 visitors. Tickets sell out 2-3 weeks in advance. Book immediately at teamlab.art with specific date/time

urgent.label: Reserve Hakone round-trip express bus
urgent.note: Limited seats available, especially for groups of 6. Book through Odakyu Transport or travel agency 5-7 days before travel date

urgent.label: Book izakaya and karaoke group dinners
urgent.note: Book Hoppy Street establishments (Asakusa), Memory Lane (Shinjuku), and karaoke chains (Shidax, Big Echo) through Tabelog or directly with hotels

urgent.label: Reserve high-end sushi dinner in Ginza
urgent.note: Michelin-starred or highly-rated sushi restaurants require 7-10 days advance booking. Book through restaurant directly or via Tabelog

urgent.label: Confirm Mount Fuji weather and visibility
urgent.note: Check Mount Fuji webcams and weather forecast 2 days before trip. Visibility is best in early morning and after rain. May need to reschedule day 6
